summary:
The articular disc of the temporomandibular joint (TMJ) typically has a central thinning that allows for greater flexibility during functions such as chewing and speaking. This characteristic is supported by anatomical studies showing consistent patterns across various human populations. However, variations exist depending on individual differences and aging processes which can influence disc structure over time.
